title: PSU comparison for DACs
full_text: I am building power supplies for Hagerman Cornet3 and Noir. I decided to use Sigma 11 boards for the permanent 9V and 24V supplies, but I also discussed temporary battery options while the boards are being built. Temporary candidates included LiFePO4 packs and Eneloop AA setups.
thoughts: I decided to use Sigma 11 PSUs for Cornet3 (9V) and Noir (24V).
I was still considering temporary LiFePO4 and Eneloop AA battery options while the permanent PSUs were being built.

title: Graph augmentation architecture
full_text: For OB1 graph augmentation, I want PostgreSQL to stay canonical and Neo4j to be a derived sidecar. I do not want Neo4j to become the write path.
thoughts: I decided to keep PostgreSQL as the canonical OB1 store and use Neo4j only as a derived sidecar graph.

title: Troubleshooting AWS CodeCommit on macOS
full_text: AWS CodeCommit authentication on macOS kept failing. I had not resolved whether the problem was the credential helper, keychain state, or the AWS profile wiring. I was still debugging it.
thoughts: I was still debugging why AWS CodeCommit authentication was failing on macOS.

title: Telephoto lenses for Fuji GFX
full_text: I was comparing telephoto strategies for Fuji GFX. I considered adapted Canon EF glass, native GF lenses, and whether the tradeoffs in cost and weight were worth it.
thoughts: I compared telephoto strategies for Fuji GFX, including adapted Canon EF glass and native GF lenses.

title: Deleting eSIM on iPhone
full_text: This was generic how-to guidance about deleting an eSIM on iPhone and did not capture a personal choice, project state, or durable preference.
thoughts: To delete an eSIM on iPhone, go to Settings, open Cellular, and remove the plan.

title: Calculating LT3015 voltage regulator output values
full_text: I worked out the LT3015 resistor values needed to get 12V and 18V outputs for an audio power supply build, and I planned to use those values in the board implementation.
thoughts: I worked out LT3015 output resistor values for 12V and 18V rails and planned to use them in the audio PSU build.
